danger_signals.visual_form: Colorful geometric figure moving erratically in playfield
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate life loss if Qix touches marker while drawing Stix (uncompleted line)

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemies traversing playfield edges and unfinished Stix
danger_signals.meaning: Life loss on contact with marker; escalates to Super Sparx that can chase along incomplete lines

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual fuse appearing and burning along drawn line toward marker
danger_signals.meaning: Indicates imminent death if marker does not move; triggered by stopping mid-draw or entering dead-end position ('Spiral Death Trap')

danger_signals.visual_form: Sparx transformation after meter countdown completes
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated threat level; enemies gain ability to pursue marker along incomplete Stix, increasing danger

safety_signals.visual_form: Diamond-shaped marker positioned on rectangle boundaries or completed captured areas
safety_signals.meaning: Safe from Qix collision; Qix does not harm marker when on edges or traversing captured territory

safety_signals.visual_form: Colored regions (orange-red for Slow Draw, other colors for Fast Draw) filling captured sections
safety_signals.meaning: Claimed territory; marker can traverse these safely; Qix and Sparx cannot penetrate filled areas

safety_signals.visual_form: Fuse visual retracting when marker resumes movement
safety_signals.meaning: Immediate danger averted; continuous movement prevents fuse activation

faction_indicators.color_identity: Diamond-shaped marker; variable fill colors (orange-red for Slow Draw bonus areas)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Drawn lines (Stix) representing player territory claims; filled regions showing player control

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Abstract stick form in graceful, unpredictable motion
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Qix presence throughout unfilled playfield; splits into two Qixes at level three, creating dual-threat zones

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Sparx (normal edge-traversing enemies) vs. Super Sparx (mutated, enhanced-capability variants)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Released on meter countdown; occupy playfield edges and pursue incomplete Stix; signal escalation via transformation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Single Qix in playfield; Sparx enemies present on edges; relatively sparse threat density
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Qix performs graceful, unpredictable motions; Sparx traverse only completed edges; fuse mechanic introduces tension

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Playfield splits into regions with two Qixes; increased Sparx count; visual density increases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dual Qix require territorial division strategy; additional Sparx spawn; Super Sparx mutations activate on meter completion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple Super Sparx visible; meter counts down to new mutations; restricted safe zones as claimed territory increases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Super Sparx actively chase marker along incomplete Stix; reduced maneuverability for player; escalated speed and threat coordination

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Percentage meter shows progress toward 75% (adjustable 50-90%) capture; bonus scoring visualization
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player incentivized to exceed threshold for bonus; Qix splitting grants multiplier bonus for subsequent levels

environmental_storytelling.example: Filled color regions directly represent player conquest and control expansion; color choice (orange-red for Slow Draw) communicates risk/reward strategy choice
Sparx count increases and mutates to Super Sparx as levels progress; visual field becomes more crowded, communicating difficulty spike without explicit text
Countdown meter at top of screen visually communicates imminent threat (additional Sparx release, Super Sparx mutation); creates tension through visible timer
Qix and marker rendered as simple geometric forms (stick figure, diamond) in minimalist style; reduces visual noise, emphasizes movement patterns and collision risk
Fuse visual burning along incomplete line directly communicates consequence for stopping; 'Spiral Death Trap' naming in attract mode primes player expectations for mechanical danger

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Graceful, unpredictable motion within rectangle; will not actively seek marker; does not harm marker on edges or completed territory
Represents collision hazard if it touches marker or exposed Stix before line completion; immediate life loss
Two Qixes appear simultaneously; each occupies separate territorial region; splitting both Qixes into divided sections completes level and grants multiplier bonus
Traverses only playfield edges and completed area boundaries; cannot enter incomplete Stix; predictable path-following
Can pursue marker along incomplete Stix lines; enhanced threat capability; activated on meter countdown completion
Safe from all threats; can move freely along boundaries and filled regions
Vulnerable state; subject to collision with Qix, Sparx, Super Sparx, and fuse burn mechanic; cannot cross or backtrack along own line
Burns along drawn line toward marker at constant rate; reaching marker causes life loss; disappears if marker resumes movement

notes: Qix uses minimalist abstract visual design to communicate world-state and threat through geometric motion, color-coded territory claims, and escalating enemy density. The game employs no text-based warnings; all danger and safety signals are conveyed through visual mechanics (collision geometry, fuse burn animation, meter countdown). The Qix entity itself is a pure threat indicator—its unpredictable graceful motion contrasts with safe geometric filled regions, creating intuitive visual risk assessment. Difficulty escalation is communicated via enemy count increase, mutation visuals (Super Sparx transformation), and playfield division (dual Qix at level 3), not explicit level markers.
